questions.q: Jawaharlal Nehru became independent India's first holder of which office?
questions.explanation: Nehru became the first Prime Minister of independent India in 1947.

questions.q: By which affectionate title was Nehru widely known?
questions.explanation: Nehru was popularly addressed as Pandit Ji, reflecting his Kashmiri Brahmin background.

questions.q: Which famous speech did Nehru deliver at the moment of India's independence?
questions.explanation: Nehru delivered the celebrated 'Tryst with Destiny' speech as India became independent at midnight on 15 August 1947.

questions.q: Who was Jawaharlal Nehru's father, himself a prominent Congress leader?
questions.explanation: Nehru's father was Motilal Nehru, a leading lawyer and Congress president.

questions.q: Nehru was a close associate of which towering leader of the freedom movement?
questions.explanation: Nehru was a foremost Congress leader and a close associate of Mahatma Gandhi.

questions.q: Which political value, central to the modern Indian state, did Nehru strongly champion?
questions.explanation: Nehru was a firm champion of secularism alongside complete independence and modern development.

questions.q: In which city was Jawaharlal Nehru born in 1889?
questions.explanation: Nehru was born in Allahabad in 1889 into the prominent Nehru family.

questions.q: Nehru's daughter, who herself later became Prime Minister of India, was?
questions.explanation: Nehru's daughter Indira Gandhi went on to become Prime Minister of India.

questions.q: Nehru presided over the Congress session of 1929 that adopted which historic goal?
questions.explanation: Nehru presided over the 1929 Lahore session, which declared Purna Swaraj, or complete independence, as the Congress goal.

questions.q: On what date did Nehru deliver his 'Tryst with Destiny' speech and take office as Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: Nehru delivered the speech at midnight on 15 August 1947 as India became independent.

questions.q: At which famous British university did Nehru study before becoming a barrister?
questions.explanation: Nehru studied at Trinity College, Cambridge, before training as a barrister in London.

questions.q: Nehru's prison-written book addressed to his daughter was titled?
questions.explanation: 'Glimpses of World History' was a collection of letters Nehru wrote from prison to his daughter Indira.

questions.q: Which influential book did Nehru write during his imprisonment at Ahmednagar Fort in the 1940s?
questions.explanation: Nehru wrote 'The Discovery of India' while imprisoned at Ahmednagar Fort during the 1940s.

questions.q: Nehru was first elected president of the Indian National Congress in which year?
questions.explanation: Nehru first became Congress president in 1929, presiding over the historic Lahore session.

questions.q: Nehru presided over the 1936 Congress session held at which village, the first Congress session held in a rural setting?
questions.explanation: Nehru presided over the Faizpur session held in December 1936, the first Congress session convened in a village; he had presided over the Lucknow session earlier that year.

questions.q: Which radical leftist group founded within the Congress in 1934 did Nehru sympathise with?
questions.explanation: Nehru sympathised with and supported the Congress Socialist Party, founded in 1934, reflecting his leftist economic outlook.

questions.q: Which economic model did Nehru promote for independent India, emphasising state-led planning?
options: A planned, mixed economy with Five-Year Plans
questions.explanation: Nehru championed a planned, mixed economy and introduced Five-Year Plans to guide India's development.

questions.q: Nehru was a leading architect of which foreign-policy stance during the Cold War?
questions.explanation: Nehru was a principal architect of the Non-Aligned Movement, keeping India apart from the Cold War blocs.

questions.q: Nehru took part in the Civil Disobedience Movement and was repeatedly imprisoned. Which decade saw the bulk of these jailings?
questions.explanation: Nehru spent long stretches in British jails during the 1930s and 1940s for his role in mass movements.

questions.q: Nehru chaired the National Planning Committee set up by the Congress in 1938. What was its purpose?
options: To negotiate with the Muslim League
To plan India's future economic development
questions.explanation: Nehru chaired the National Planning Committee in 1938, an early effort to plan India's economic development.

questions.q: Nehru headed the interim government formed in 1946 with what designation?
questions.explanation: Nehru led the 1946 interim government as Vice-President of the Viceroy's Executive Council, effectively its head.

questions.q: Which autobiography did Nehru publish in 1936, written largely in prison?
options: The Story of My Experiments with Truth
questions.explanation: Nehru's 'An Autobiography', also published as 'Toward Freedom', appeared in 1936 and was written largely during imprisonment.

questions.q: Nehru's first major exposure to mass peasant conditions came during his work among cultivators in which region in the early 1920s?
options: The kisan movement in the United Provinces
questions.explanation: Nehru's early involvement with the peasant (kisan) movement in the United Provinces shaped his social outlook in the 1920s.

questions.q: Nehru was associated with which 1928 report, proposing a constitutional scheme for India and drafted under his father's chairmanship?
questions.explanation: The 1928 Nehru Report was chaired by Motilal Nehru, with Jawaharlal among the younger leaders involved, proposing a constitutional framework for India.

questions.q: At the 1927 Brussels Congress against Imperialism, Nehru represented Indian nationalism alongside other anti-colonial delegates. What did this reflect about his outlook?
options: His support for the British Empire
questions.explanation: Nehru attended the 1927 Brussels Congress against Imperialism, reflecting his internationalist and anti-imperialist outlook.

questions.q: Nehru visited the Soviet Union in 1927, an experience that influenced his views on?
questions.explanation: Nehru's 1927 visit to the Soviet Union deepened his interest in economic planning and socialist ideas.

questions.q: In an anonymous 1937 article titled 'The Rashtrapati', who critiqued Nehru's own potential for authoritarianism?
options: Nehru himself, writing under a pseudonym
questions.explanation: Nehru wrote the anonymous essay 'The Rashtrapati' under the pen-name Chanakya, warning against the dangers of his own popularity becoming authoritarian.

questions.q: Nehru moved the historic Objectives Resolution in the Constituent Assembly in December 1946. This resolution later formed the basis of which part of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Nehru's Objectives Resolution of December 1946 laid down the guiding principles that became the basis of the Preamble to the Constitution.

questions.q: Which institution of higher technical and scientific research did Nehru strongly promote after independence as part of building a modern India?
questions.explanation: Nehru promoted scientific and technical institutions such as the Indian Institutes of Technology as part of his vision of a modern, industrial India.

questions.q: Nehru served continuously as Prime Minister until his death. In which year did he die in office?
questions.explanation: Nehru remained Prime Minister from 1947 until his death in office in 1964.
